creator: msl0000023508@gmail.com
text:
Apparently the upstream code has changed a lot since I filed this report. By looking the commit history of that source file, I can confirm that this buggy logic has been removed entirely, by this commit:

https://code.videolan.org/videolan/x264/-/commit/8a43cc145aa1b97705b18b2e8bc2260071eb3656?view=parallel

However considering x265 is based on x264, I suspect the same bug may also affect that package as well (when being used as library). For example, I can find an misuse of nice(3) there:

https://bitbucket.org/multicoreware/x265_git/src/4.1/source/common/threadpool.cpp#lines-121

Untested because I don't have a demand for H.265 encoding.
